 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-hlz9","title":"Test Coverage Reporting with cargo-llvm-cov","description":"# Test Coverage Reporting with cargo-llvm-cov

## What
Integrate cargo-llvm-cov for test coverage analysis, generating:
- Line coverage reports
- Branch coverage reports
- HTML reports for visualization
- Codecov/Coveralls integration for CI

## Why
Without coverage reporting, we cannot:
- Identify untested code paths
- Track coverage over time
- Ensure new code has tests
- Set coverage gates in CI

## Technical Design

### Installation
```bash
# Install llvm-tools and cargo-llvm-cov
rustup component add llvm-tools-preview
cargo install cargo-llvm-cov
```

### Coverage Script
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/coverage.sh

set -euo pipefail

REPORT_DIR="target/coverage"
mkdir -p "$REPORT_DIR"

echo "📊 Generating coverage report..."

# Clean previous coverage data
cargo llvm-cov clean --workspace

# Run tests with coverage instrumentation
cargo llvm-cov --all-features \\
    --workspace \\
    --ignore-filename-regex "tests/" \\
    --html \\
    --output-dir "$REPORT_DIR"

# Also generate lcov format for CI upload
cargo llvm-cov --all-features \\
    --workspace \\
    --ignore-filename-regex "tests/" \\
    --lcov \\
    --output-path "$REPORT_DIR/lcov.info"

# Generate JSON summary
cargo llvm-cov --all-features \\
    --workspace \\
    --ignore-filename-regex "tests/" \\
    --json \\
    --output-path "$REPORT_DIR/coverage.json"

echo ""
echo "✓ Coverage report generated:"
echo "  HTML: $REPORT_DIR/html/index.html"
echo "  LCOV: $REPORT_DIR/lcov.info"
echo "  JSON: $REPORT_DIR/coverage.json"

# Print summary
cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace --ignore-filename-regex "tests/"
```

### GitHub Actions Integration
```yaml
# .github/workflows/coverage.yml
name: Coverage

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  coverage:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Install Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-action@nightly
        with:
          components: llvm-tools-preview
          
      - name: Install cargo-llvm-cov
        uses: taiki-e/install-action@cargo-llvm-cov
        
      - name: Generate coverage
        run: |
          cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace \\
            --ignore-filename-regex "tests/" \\
            --codecov --output-path codecov.json
            
      - name: Upload to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          files: codecov.json
          fail_ci_if_error: true
```

### Coverage Configuration
```toml
# .cargo/config.toml
[env]
# Exclude test utilities from coverage
CARGO_LLVM_COV_EXCLUDE = "tests/*,**/test*.rs"
```

### Coverage Gate
Add to CI to fail if coverage drops:
```yaml
- name: Check coverage threshold
  run: |
    coverage=$(cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace --json | jq -r ".data[0].totals.lines.percent")
    if (( $(echo "$coverage < 80" | bc -l) )); then
      echo "Coverage $coverage% is below 80% threshold"
      exit 1
    fi
```

### Uncovered Code Report
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/coverage-uncovered.sh
# Show functions with 0% coverage

cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace \\
    --ignore-filename-regex "tests/" \\
    --show-missing-lines \\
    --fail-under-lines 80
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cargo-llvm-cov installed in CI
- [ ] HTML coverage report generated
- [ ] LCOV format for external tools
- [ ] Codecov integration working
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] 80% line coverage threshold
- [ ] scripts/coverage.sh works locally
- [ ] Coverage gate in PR checks

## Dependencies
- cargo-llvm-cov
- Codecov account (free for open source)

## Considerations
- Coverage instrumentation adds ~2x build time
- Run coverage on nightly for best compatibility
- Exclude test code from coverage metrics
- Consider branch coverage later (harder to achieve)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-hr0z4","title":"[MEDIUM] reality-check: README --since -7d example fails (clap rejects dash-prefixed value)","description":"## Claim (README.md:1169-1173)
\`\`\`
# All equivalent for \"last week\"
cass search \"bug\" --since -7d
cass search \"bug\" --since \"-1w\"
cass search \"bug\" --days 7
\`\`\`

And README.md:1157 advertises relative time values:
> | **Relative** | \`-7d\`, \`-24h\`, \`-30m\`, \`-1w\` | Days, hours, minutes, weeks ago |

## Reality
The first two examples (\`--since -7d\`, \`--since \"-1w\"\`) BOTH fail:

\`\`\`
\$ cass search \"bug\" --robot --since -7d
{\"status\":\"error\",\"error\":\"error: unexpected argument '-7' found\\n\\n  tip: to pass '-7' as a value, use '-- -7'\\n\\n...\"}

\$ cass search \"bug\" --robot --since \"-1w\"
{\"status\":\"error\",\"error\":\"error: unexpected argument '-1' found\\n\\n...\"}
\`\`\`

clap parses \`-7d\` / \`-1w\` as attempted flags starting with \`-\`, not as the value of \`--since\`. Shell quoting doesn't help — clap inspects the argv string before unquoting considerations.

The third example (\`--days 7\`) works. The equals form \`--since=-7d\` also works. But the README explicitly documents the space form and calls all three \"equivalent\".

## Minimal repro
\`\`\`bash
cass search \"bug\" --since -7d     # FAILS exit 2
cass search \"bug\" --since \"-1w\"   # FAILS exit 2
cass search \"bug\" --since=-7d     # works
cass search \"bug\" --days 7         # works
cass search \"bug\" --since \"yesterday\"  # works (no leading dash)
\`\`\`

## Suggested fix
Two options:

1. **Update README to match reality.** Replace the space-form examples with equals-form:
   \`\`\`bash
   # All equivalent for \"last week\"
   cass search \"bug\" --since=-7d     # equals form required for dash-prefixed values
   cass search \"bug\" --since=-1w
   cass search \"bug\" --days 7
   \`\`\`

2. **Use clap's \`allow_hyphen_values(true)\`** on \`--since\` and \`--until\` so the space form works:
   \`\`\`rust
   #[arg(long, allow_hyphen_values = true)]
   pub since: Option<String>,
   \`\`\`
   This is the cleaner fix — it makes the documented UX actually work and avoids surprising agents that copy-paste the README example.

Option 2 is straightforward. Grep for \`pub since\` / \`pub until\` in src/lib.rs and add \`allow_hyphen_values = true\` to the \`#[arg]\` attrs. Any other relative-time flags (\`--after\`, etc.) should get the same treatment.

Severity: MEDIUM — the documented quickstart example produces exit-2 error for agents copy-pasting the relative-time syntax; non-obvious equals-form workaround.

Labels: cli, documentation, reality-check, clap-parsing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:24:40.970670018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:19:43.622509607Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:19:43.622144714Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit e734d92a (allow_hyphen_values = true on every --since/--until arg across Search, Timeline, AnalyticsCommon, and related clap structs). Verified: `cass search bug --robot --since -7d` and `--since "-1w"` now produce clean missing-index JSON envelopes instead of 'error: unexpected argument -7 found'. README.md:1171-1172 examples now work as documented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-hv9t","title":"Epic: Interactive Remote Sources Setup Wizard","description":"# Epic: Interactive Remote Sources Setup Wizard

## Vision
Transform cass's multi-machine experience from manual configuration to a seamless, 
guided setup process. Users should be able to aggregate their coding agent session 
history from multiple machines with minimal friction.

## Current Pain Points
1. Users must manually edit sources.toml to add remote machines
2. Users must manually install cass on each remote machine
3. Users must manually run `cass index` on each remote
4. Users must manually run `cass sources sync` to pull data
5. No visibility into what data exists on remotes before configuring

## Target Experience
```
$ cass sources setup

Discovering SSH hosts from ~/.ssh/config...
Found 5 hosts

Probing hosts (this may take a moment)...
  css ✓  csd ✓  trj ✓  yto ✓  fmd ✗

Select hosts to configure as sources:
  [x] css (209.145.54.164) - cass v0.1.50 ✓ | 1,234 sessions
  [x] csd (144.126.137.164) - cass v0.1.49 ✓ | 567 sessions
  [ ] trj (100.91.120.17) - cass not found | Claude, Cursor data detected
  [x] yto (37.187.75.150) - cass not found | Claude data detected
  [ ] fmd (51.222.245.56) - unreachable

[space] toggle  [a] all  [n] none  [/] search  [enter] confirm

→ Install cass on 1 selected host without it? [Y/n] y
  Installing on yto via cargo binstall... ████████████████░░░░ 80%
  ✓ Installed cass 0.1.50 on yto

→ Run initial indexing on yto? [Y/n] y
  Indexing yto... ████████████████████ 100%
  ✓ Indexed 234 sessions on yto

Configuration preview:
  css: ~/.claude/projects, ~/.codex/sessions
       /data/projects → ~/projects
  csd: ~/.claude/projects, ~/.codex/sessions
       /data/projects → ~/projects
  yto: ~/.claude/projects
       /home/ubuntu → ~/projects

[✓ Save configuration]

→ Backing up existing sources.toml...
→ Adding 3 sources to configuration...
→ Syncing data from remotes...
  css ████████████████████ 100%
  csd ████████████████████ 100%
  yto ████████████████████ 100%

✓ Setup complete! 1,801 sessions now searchable.

Run 'cass search <query>' to search across all machines.
```

## Key Principles
1. **Opt-in by default**: Never auto-configure without explicit user selection
2. **Graceful degradation**: Work with partial connectivity/failures
3. **Transparency**: Show what will happen before doing it
4. **Idempotent**: Safe to run multiple times
5. **Non-destructive**: Never delete existing configuration
6. **Resumable**: Interrupted setup can be resumed from saved state
7. **Customizable**: Allow editing paths/mappings before saving

## Technical Approach
- Use interactive TUI library (dialoguer/inquire/ratatui - evaluate in subtask)
- SSH probing via single-command script for efficiency
- Parallel probing with progress display
- Remote installation via cargo binstall (fast) or cargo install (reliable)
- Leverage existing sync engine for data transfer
- State persistence for resume capability

## Error Handling Strategy

### SSH Connection Failures
- **Timeout**: Show as "unreachable", allow user to retry or skip
- **Auth failure**: Show specific error, suggest `ssh-add` if key issue
- **Host key**: Accept new keys automatically (StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new)
- **Partial failures**: Continue with working hosts, summarize failures at end

### Installation Failures
- **No cargo**: Offer binary download alternative
- **Compilation error**: Show log excerpt, suggest checking dependencies
- **Disk full**: Detect upfront via probe, warn before attempting
- **Network timeout**: Installation runs in background, polls for completion

### Indexing Failures
- **Index timeout**: Runs in background, polls for progress
- **Partial data**: Warn but continue (better to have some data than none)
- **Permissions**: Warn if some paths inaccessible

### Config Failures
- **Invalid TOML**: Validate round-trip before writing
- **Backup failed**: Abort config write, preserve existing
- **Merge conflict**: Skip existing sources, show what was skipped

### Recovery
- All progress saved to `~/.config/cass/setup_state.json`
- Resume with `cass sources setup --resume`
- Clear stuck state with `cass sources setup --resume --force`

## Edge Cases

### Many Hosts (50+)
- Search/filter capability essential
- Virtual scroll for large lists
- "Select first N matching" for batch operations

### Slow Networks
- Longer timeouts for overseas hosts
- Progress indication during long operations
- Background operations with polling

### Partial Access
- Some hosts accessible, others not
- Clear indication of what's working
- Continue with available hosts

### Existing Configuration
- Detect already-configured hosts
- Show differently in UI (grayed out / badge)
- Don't duplicate, offer to update if wanted

### Mixed cass Versions
- Remote has older cass than local
- Warn about potential compatibility issues
- Offer to upgrade remote

### No Agent Data Found
- Host reachable but no session data detected
- Still allow configuration (user may know of custom paths)
- Lower in selection priority

### Interrupted Setup
- Ctrl+C during probe: Safe, nothing changed
- Ctrl+C during install: State saved, resume continues
- Ctrl+C during config: State saved, resume skips install
- Ctrl+C during sync: Partial sync, re-run completes

### Concurrent Runs
- Lock file prevents concurrent setup runs
- Clear error message if locked

## Success Metrics
- Time to first cross-machine search: < 5 minutes from fresh install
- Zero manual file editing required
- Works with any SSH-accessible machine
- 90%+ of users succeed without consulting docs

## Dependencies
- Existing SSH config discovery (done)
- Existing sync engine with tilde expansion (done)
- Interactive TUI crate (to be added - see subtask tlk6)

## Out of Scope (Future Work)
- Windows remote support (SSH on Windows is complex)
- Non-SSH transports (HTTP API, etc.)
- Automatic periodic sync scheduling
- Remote machine auto-discovery (mDNS, etc.)
- GUI/web interface

## Subtasks
1. **tlk6**: Add dialoguer crate for interactive TUI
2. **vxe2**: Implement SSH host probing for cass status
3. **rnjt**: Build interactive host selection UI
4. **o6ax**: Implement remote cass installation via SSH
5. **x4sj**: Implement remote index triggering
6. **wygt**: Auto-configure sources.toml from selection
7. **dbdl**: Implement 'cass sources setup' command (orchestrator)
8. **jjal**: Add unit and integration tests
9. **22j1**: Update documentation

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-hwtz","title":"Task: E2E integration test suite for semantic search pipeline","description":"Comprehensive end-to-end tests for the cass semantic search pipeline with all embedding/reranking combinations, daemon modes, and edge cases.

## Test Script Setup
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# tests/e2e/test_semantic_pipeline.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_FILE="tests/e2e/results/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p tests/e2e/results

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $*"; ((PASSES++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $*"; ((FAILURES++)); FAILED_TESTS+=("$*"); }

PASSES=0
FAILURES=0
FAILED_TESTS=()
```

## Environment Logging (MANDATORY)
```bash
log_environment() {
    log "========================================="
    log "E2E Test Environment"
    log "========================================="
    log "Timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)"
    log "OS: $(uname -a)"
    log "CPU: $(nproc) cores"
    log "Rust: $(rustc --version)"
    log "cass version: $(cass --version 2>&1)"
    log "PWD: $(pwd)"
    log "CASS_* env vars:"
    env | grep -E '^CASS_' | sort || log "  (none set)"
    log "========================================="
}
```

## Test Categories

### 1. Index Building Tests
```bash
test_index_build() {
    log "=== Test: Index Building ==="
    local test_data="$TEST_WORKSPACE/sessions"
    
    # Build semantic index
    cass index --semantic --data-dir "$test_data" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    [[ $? -eq 0 ]] && pass "cass index --semantic" || fail "cass index failed"
    
    # Verify index files exist
    [[ -f "$test_data/.cass/vectors.bin" ]] && pass "vectors.bin created" || fail "vectors.bin missing"
}
```

### 2. Search Quality Tests
```bash
test_semantic_search() {
    log "=== Test: Semantic Search ==="
    
    # Search with known query
    local result=$(cass search "authentication middleware" --semantic --json)
    local count=$(echo "$result" | jq '.results | length')
    
    [[ "$count" -gt 0 ]] && pass "Got $count results" || fail "No results"
    
    # Check latency
    local latency=$(echo "$result" | jq '.latency_ms')
    log "Search latency: ${latency}ms"
    (( $(echo "$latency < 500" | bc -l) )) && pass "Latency OK" || fail "Slow search"
}
```

### 3. Two-Tier Search Tests
```bash
test_two_tier_search() {
    log "=== Test: Two-Tier Progressive Search ==="
    
    # Search with two-tier
    local result=$(cass search "refactoring discussion" --two-tier --json --verbose 2>&1)
    
    local fast_ms=$(echo "$result" | jq -r ".phases[0].latency_ms // 0")
    [[ "$fast_ms" -lt 10 ]] && pass "Fast phase <10ms" || fail "Fast phase slow: ${fast_ms}ms"
    
    local phases=$(echo "$result" | jq ".phases | length")
    [[ "$phases" -ge 2 ]] && pass "Got refinement phase" || log "INFO: Refinement skipped"
}
```

### 4. Daemon Integration Tests
```bash
test_daemon_mode() {
    log "=== Test: Daemon Mode ==="
    
    # Start daemon
    cass daemon start && pass "daemon start" || fail "daemon start"
    sleep 2
    
    # Check status
    cass daemon status && pass "daemon status" || fail "daemon status"
    
    # Search via daemon
    local result=$(cass search "test" --semantic --daemon --json)
    [[ $? -eq 0 ]] && pass "search via daemon" || fail "search via daemon"
    
    # Stop daemon
    cass daemon stop && pass "daemon stop" || fail "daemon stop"
}
```

### 5. Reranking Tests
```bash
test_reranking() {
    log "=== Test: Reranking ==="
    
    local result=$(cass search "test query" --semantic --rerank --json)
    local has_rerank=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.[0].rerank_score // empty')
    
    [[ -n "$has_rerank" ]] && pass "Rerank scores present" || fail "No rerank scores"
}
```

### 6. Fallback Tests
```bash
test_fallback() {
    log "=== Test: Fallback Behavior ==="
    
    # Stop daemon if running
    cass daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Search should still work via direct inference
    local result=$(cass search "test" --semantic --json 2>&1)
    [[ $? -eq 0 ]] && pass "Fallback to direct" || fail "Fallback failed"
}
```

## Summary Report
```bash
log "========================="
log "E2E Test Summary"
log "========================="
log "Passed: $PASSES"
log "Failed: $FAILURES"
if [[ ${#FAILED_TESTS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    log "Failed tests:"
    for t in "${FAILED_TESTS[@]}"; do
        log "  - $t"
    done
fi
exit $FAILURES
```

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-i5wp","title":"P6.14: Master E2E Test Suite with Comprehensive Logging","description":"# P6.14: Master E2E Test Suite with Comprehensive Logging

## Goal
Create a comprehensive end-to-end test suite that validates the entire export-to-view workflow with detailed logging, enabling confident releases and rapid debugging of issues.

## Why This Task is Critical

Individual component tests verify parts work in isolation. The Master E2E suite verifies:
- All components integrate correctly
- Real-world workflows complete successfully
- Performance meets requirements
- Edge cases are handled
- Regressions are caught before release

## Test Suite Structure

### Test Configuration

```rust
// tests/e2e/config.rs
use std::path::PathBuf;

pub struct E2EConfig {
    pub test_data_dir: PathBuf,
    pub output_dir: PathBuf,
    pub log_level: tracing::Level,
    pub browser: BrowserConfig,
    pub timeout_ms: u64,
}

pub struct BrowserConfig {
    pub headless: bool,
    pub browser_type: String,  // "chromium", "firefox", "webkit"
    pub viewport: (u32, u32),
}

impl Default for E2EConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            test_data_dir: PathBuf::from("tests/fixtures/e2e"),
            output_dir: PathBuf::from("target/e2e"),
            log_level: tracing::Level::DEBUG,
            browser: BrowserConfig {
                headless: std::env::var("CI").is_ok(),
                browser_type: "chromium".into(),
                viewport: (1280, 720),
            },
            timeout_ms: 30000,
        }
    }
}
```

### Master Test Runner

```rust
// tests/e2e/runner.rs
use tracing::{info, debug, error, instrument, span, Level};
use std::time::Instant;

pub struct E2ETestRunner {
    config: E2EConfig,
    results: Vec<TestResult>,
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct TestResult {
    pub name: String,
    pub status: TestStatus,
    pub duration_ms: u64,
    pub logs: Vec<String>,
    pub screenshots: Vec<PathBuf>,
    pub error: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
pub enum TestStatus {
    Passed,
    Failed,
    Skipped,
    TimedOut,
}

impl E2ETestRunner {
    #[instrument(skip(self))]
    pub async fn run_all(&mut self) -> E2EReport {
        info!("Starting Master E2E Test Suite");
        let suite_start = Instant::now();
        
        // Workflow tests
        self.run_test("Full Export Workflow", Self::test_full_export_workflow).await;
        self.run_test("Password Authentication", Self::test_password_auth).await;
        self.run_test("QR Code Authentication", Self::test_qr_auth).await;
        self.run_test("Search Functionality", Self::test_search).await;
        self.run_test("Conversation Viewing", Self::test_conversation_view).await;
        self.run_test("Offline Mode", Self::test_offline_mode).await;
        self.run_test("Large Archive Handling", Self::test_large_archive).await;
        self.run_test("Secret Detection", Self::test_secret_detection).await;
        self.run_test("Redaction", Self::test_redaction).await;
        self.run_test("Multi-Key-Slot", Self::test_multi_key_slot).await;
        self.run_test("Recovery Key", Self::test_recovery_key).await;
        self.run_test("Cross-Browser Compat", Self::test_cross_browser).await;
        self.run_test("Accessibility", Self::test_accessibility).await;
        
        let total_duration = suite_start.elapsed();
        
        E2EReport {
            results: std::mem::take(&mut self.results),
            total_duration_ms: total_duration.as_millis() as u64,
            passed: self.results.iter().filter(|r| r.status == TestStatus::Passed).count(),
            failed: self.results.iter().filter(|r| r.status == TestStatus::Failed).count(),
        }
    }

    async fn run_test<F, Fut>(&mut self, name: &str, test_fn: F)
    where
        F: FnOnce(&Self) -> Fut,
        Fut: std::future::Future<Output = Result<(), E2EError>>,
    {
        let span = span!(Level::INFO, "test", name = name);
        let _guard = span.enter();
        
        info!("Starting test: {}", name);
        let start = Instant::now();
        
        let result = match tokio::time::timeout(
            Duration::from_millis(self.config.timeout_ms),
            test_fn(self)
        ).await {
            Ok(Ok(())) => {
                info!("Test passed: {}", name);
                TestResult {
                    name: name.into(),
                    status: TestStatus::Passed,
                    duration_ms: start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
                    ..Default::default()
                }
            }
            Ok(Err(e)) => {
                error!("Test failed: {} - {:?}", name, e);
                TestResult {
                    name: name.into(),
                    status: TestStatus::Failed,
                    error: Some(format!("{:?}", e)),
                    duration_ms: start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
                    ..Default::default()
                }
            }
            Err(_) => {
                error!("Test timed out: {}", name);
                TestResult {
                    name: name.into(),
                    status: TestStatus::TimedOut,
                    error: Some("Timed out".into()),
                    duration_ms: self.config.timeout_ms,
                    ..Default::default()
                }
            }
        };
        
        self.results.push(result);
    }
}
```

### Full Export Workflow Test

```rust
#[instrument]
async fn test_full_export_workflow(&self) -> Result<(), E2EError> {
    info!("=== Full Export Workflow Test ===");
    
    // Step 1: Generate test data
    debug!("Step 1: Generating test data");
    let sessions = generate_test_sessions(100);
    info!("Generated {} test sessions", sessions.len());
    
    // Step 2: Export with encryption
    debug!("Step 2: Exporting with encryption");
    let password = "test-password-e2e";
    let export_result = export_encrypted(&sessions, password)?;
    info!(
        "Export complete: {} bytes, {} key slots",
        export_result.ciphertext.len(),
        export_result.config.key_slots.len()
    );
    
    // Step 3: Build static site
    debug!("Step 3: Building static site");
    let bundle = BundleBuilder::new(default_config())
        .build(&export_result)?;
    info!("Bundle created: {}", bundle.site_dir.display());
    
    // Step 4: Start local server
    debug!("Step 4: Starting local server");
    let server = LocalServer::start(&bundle.site_dir, 0)?;
    info!("Server started at {}", server.url());
    
    // Step 5: Load in browser
    debug!("Step 5: Loading in browser");
    let page = self.new_page().await?;
    page.goto(&server.url()).await?;
    
    // Step 6: Verify password prompt
    debug!("Step 6: Verifying password prompt");
    let password_input = page.wait_for_selector("#password-input", 5000).await?;
    assert!(password_input.is_visible().await?);
    info!("Password prompt displayed");
    
    // Step 7: Enter password
    debug!("Step 7: Entering password");
    password_input.fill(password).await?;
    page.click("#unlock-button").await?;
    
    // Step 8: Wait for unlock
    debug!("Step 8: Waiting for unlock");
    page.wait_for_selector(".search-container", 10000).await?;
    info!("Archive unlocked successfully");
    
    // Step 9: Perform search
    debug!("Step 9: Performing search");
    let search_input = page.query_selector("#search-input").await?;
    search_input.fill("test").await?;
    search_input.press("Enter").await?;
    
    // Step 10: Verify results
    debug!("Step 10: Verifying results");
    let results = page.query_selector_all(".search-result").await?;
    assert!(results.len() > 0, "Expected search results");
    info!("Found {} search results", results.len());
    
    // Step 11: Open conversation
    debug!("Step 11: Opening conversation");
    results[0].click().await?;
    page.wait_for_selector(".conversation-content", 5000).await?;
    info!("Conversation loaded");
    
    // Step 12: Verify content
    debug!("Step 12: Verifying content");
    let content = page.inner_text(".conversation-content").await?;
    assert!(!content.is_empty(), "Expected conversation content");
    info!("Content verified: {} chars", content.len());
    
    // Cleanup
    server.stop();
    
    info!("=== Full Export Workflow Test PASSED ===");
    Ok(())
}
```

### HTML Test Report

```rust
impl E2EReport {
    pub fn to_html(&self) -> String {
        format!(r#"
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>E2E Test Report</title>
    <style>
        body {{ font-family: system-ui; margin: 2rem; }}
        .passed {{ color: green; }}
        .failed {{ color: red; }}
        .summary {{ font-size: 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 2rem; }}
        .test {{ border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 1rem; margin: 0.5rem 0; }}
        .logs {{ font-family: monospace; background: #f5f5f5; padding: 1rem; overflow-x: auto; }}
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>E2E Test Report</h1>
    <div class="summary">
        <span class="passed">{passed} passed</span> / 
        <span class="failed">{failed} failed</span> / 
        {total} total ({duration}ms)
    </div>
    {test_results}
</body>
</html>
"#,
            passed = self.passed,
            failed = self.failed,
            total = self.results.len(),
            duration = self.total_duration_ms,
            test_results = self.results.iter().map(|r| r.to_html()).collect::<String>()
        )
    }
}
```

### Screenshot on Failure

```rust
async fn capture_failure_context(&self, page: &Page, test_name: &str) -> PathBuf {
    let screenshot_dir = self.config.output_dir.join("screenshots");
    fs::create_dir_all(&screenshot_dir).ok();
    
    let filename = format!("{}_{}.png", test_name.replace(" ", "_"), chrono::Utc::now().timestamp());
    let path = screenshot_dir.join(&filename);
    
    page.screenshot(ScreenshotOptions {
        path: Some(path.clone()),
        full_page: true,
    }).await.ok();
    
    info!("Captured failure screenshot: {}", path.display());
    path
}
```

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xip3o","title":"[MEDIUM] reality-check: --robot-format jsonl header-line-with-_meta claim requires --robot-meta","description":"## Claim (README.md:733-734)
\`\`\`bash
# Streaming JSONL: header line with _meta, then one hit per line
cass search \"error\" --robot-format jsonl
\`\`\`

The comment promises that \`--robot-format jsonl\` produces a header line with \`_meta\` followed by one-hit-per-line data. The example command does NOT include \`--robot-meta\`.

## Reality
\`\`\`
# Populated fixture, 3 matrix hits:

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --limit 3 | wc -l
3                       # ← 3 lines, NO header; each line is a hit

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --limit 3 | head -1 | jq 'keys | first'
\"agent\"                  # first key alphabetical on a hit, no _meta

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --robot-meta --limit 3 | wc -l
4                       # ← 4 lines: 1 header + 3 hits

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --robot-meta --limit 3 | head -1 | jq 'keys'
[\"_meta\"]                # header line IS present when --robot-meta is set
\`\`\`

So the header line is conditional on \`--robot-meta\`, which the README example omits. Agents copy-pasting the README command and parsing line 1 as \`_meta\` will actually parse a data hit as \`_meta\` — shape mismatch.

The inline help for \`--robot-format\` correctly describes this as \"optional _meta header\":
\`\`\`
jsonl: Newline-delimited JSON: one object per line with optional _meta header
\`\`\`

but the README promo text does not.

## Minimal repro
\`\`\`bash
FAKE=\$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p \$FAKE/.codex/sessions/2025/11/25
cp tests/fixtures/codex_real/sessions/2025/11/25/rollout-test.jsonl \$FAKE/.codex/sessions/2025/11/25/
XDG_DATA_HOME=\$FAKE HOME=\$FAKE CASS_IGNORE_SOURCES_CONFIG=1 \\
  CODING_AGENT_SEARCH_NO_UPDATE_PROMPT=1 cass index --full
XDG_DATA_HOME=\$FAKE HOME=\$FAKE CASS_IGNORE_SOURCES_CONFIG=1 \\
  CODING_AGENT_SEARCH_NO_UPDATE_PROMPT=1 cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --limit 3 | head -1 | jq .
# Output is a hit object (with \"agent\", \"content\", ...), NOT a header with \"_meta\".
\`\`\`

## Suggested fix
Minimal docs patch — update the README example to include \`--robot-meta\`, matching the CLI help language:

\`\`\`bash
# Streaming JSONL: one hit per line. Add --robot-meta to prepend a _meta
# header line with elapsed_ms, next_cursor, state, and index_freshness.
cass search \"error\" --robot-format jsonl               # 3 hit lines
cass search \"error\" --robot-format jsonl --robot-meta  # 1 header + 3 hit lines
\`\`\`

Alternative (code change): make \`--robot-format jsonl\` ALWAYS emit a header line to match the docs — but that's a behavior change that may break existing consumers.

Recommend the docs fix.

Severity: MEDIUM — streaming JSONL is documented as the agent-friendly parse format; an agent reading line 1 expecting \`_meta\` and getting a hit object will JSON-parse successfully but deref the wrong shape.

Labels: documentation, cli, reality-check, jsonl.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:37.734184476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:37.734184476Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xip3o","title":"[MEDIUM] reality-check: --robot-format jsonl header-line-with-_meta claim requires --robot-meta","description":"## Claim (README.md:733-734)
\`\`\`bash
# Streaming JSONL: header line with _meta, then one hit per line
cass search \"error\" --robot-format jsonl
\`\`\`

The comment promises that \`--robot-format jsonl\` produces a header line with \`_meta\` followed by one-hit-per-line data. The example command does NOT include \`--robot-meta\`.

## Reality
\`\`\`
# Populated fixture, 3 matrix hits:

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --limit 3 | wc -l

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --limit 3 | head -1 | jq 'keys | first'
\"agent\"                  # first key alphabetical on a hit, no _meta

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --robot-meta --limit 3 | wc -l
4                       # ← 4 lines: 1 header + 3 hits

\$ cass search matrix --robot-format jsonl --robot-meta --limit 3 | head -1 | jq 'keys'
[\"_meta\"]                # header line IS present when --robot-meta is set
\`\`\`

So the header line is conditional on \`--robot-meta\`, which the README example omits. Agents copy-pasting the README command and parsing line 1 as \`_meta\` will actually parse a data hit as \`_meta\` — shape mismatch.

The inline help for \`--robot-format\` correctly describes this as \"optional _meta header\":
\`\`\`
jsonl: Newline-delimited JSON: one object per line with optional _meta header
\`\`\`

but the README promo text does not.

\`\`\`

## Suggested fix
Minimal docs patch — update the README example to include \`--robot-meta\`, matching the CLI help language:

\`\`\`bash
# Streaming JSONL: one hit per line. Add --robot-meta to prepend a _meta
# header line with elapsed_ms, next_cursor, state, and index_freshness.
cass search \"error\" --robot-format jsonl               # 3 hit lines
cass search \"error\" --robot-format jsonl --robot-meta  # 1 header + 3 hit lines
\`\`\`

Alternative (code change): make \`--robot-format jsonl\` ALWAYS emit a header line to match the docs — but that's a behavior change that may break existing consumers.

Recommend the docs fix.

Severity: MEDIUM — streaming JSONL is documented as the agent-friendly parse format; an agent reading line 1 expecting \`_meta\` and getting a hit object will JSON-parse successfully but deref the wrong shape.

Labels: documentation, cli, reality-check, jsonl.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:28:37.734184476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:51:25.573765517Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:51:25.573421803Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit bb5d9652: README.md:733 example now shows both forms — 'cass search "error" --robot-format jsonl' (hits only, no header) and 'cass search "error" --robot-format jsonl --robot-meta' (1 _meta header + hits). Verified against debug binary on a populated fixture: unquoted-no-meta produces 3 lines, all hits; with --robot-meta produces 4 lines, line 1 keys=['_meta'], lines 2-4 are hits. No code change — docs now match the CLI help's 'optional _meta header' description.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xjt3","title":"[E2E] TUI smoke tests (headless) with logging","description":"# Goal\nAdd headless TUI smoke tests that exercise launch, search input, and exit paths with verbose logs.\n\n## Subtasks\n- [ ] Use existing --once / headless modes where possible.\n- [ ] Capture TUI state snapshots and log key events.\n- [ ] Validate exit codes and no panics on empty datasets.\n\n## Acceptance\n- Automated TUI smoke test runs in CI without manual interaction.\n- Logs clearly show steps and any failures.\n","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:41:02.836055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:29:21.618480Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:29:21.618343Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-xjt3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-vh1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:55Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xwfak","title":"Golden-freeze models status --json output","description":"Follow-up to u9osp (closed). u9osp landed the golden-harness infrastructure (tests/golden_robot_json.rs with UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 workflow, scrubber, assert_golden helper) plus the first golden (capabilities.json.golden). The PROVENANCE.md file listed three remaining robot-mode JSON surfaces needing similar coverage: health.json, models_status.json, robot_docs.json.

models_status.json is the easiest next slice because 'cass models status --json' reads XDG_DATA_HOME for the model cache dir, which the test harness already pins to an isolated TempDir. In that isolated environment the model is Not-Installed so the output is deterministic modulo:
- absolute path to the model_dir (already scrubbed by scrub_robot_json → [TEST_HOME])
- paths inside the 'files' list (same scrubber)
- total_size_bytes / expected_size values per file (stable per-manifest revision)
- cache_lifecycle state_code + next_step strings (stable for the not-installed state)

Add:
- tests/golden_robot_json.rs test fn models_status_json_matches_golden
- tests/golden/robot/models_status.json.golden (scrubbed)
- PROVENANCE.md entry documenting the command + scrubbed fields

DONE WHEN: rch exec cargo test --test golden_robot_json passes including the new test. Existing capabilities golden remains unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:50:17.567200398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:54:10.131366977Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:54:10.130976004Z","close_reason":"Models status golden shipped in commits 34231ff1 (test + golden) and e40d545d (PROVENANCE). tests/golden_robot_json.rs now has shared capture_robot_json helper feeding both capabilities and models_status tests; tests/golden/robot/models_status.json.golden freezes the full not-installed shape (state, state_detail, next_step, files[], cache_lifecycle). UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 → 2/2 pass, stable re-run 2/2 pass, rch cargo check --all-targets green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["golden","testing"]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-xwzp","title":"Binary release workflow for all platforms","description":"## Summary
Create GitHub Actions release workflow to build and publish binary artifacts for all platforms on tag push.

## Context
- GitHub Issue #39 requests binary artifacts for releases
- Current releases have 0 assets - users must build from source
- Need cross-compilation for Linux, macOS, Windows

## Requirements
1. Trigger on tag push (v*)
2. Build for: linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, macos-x86_64, macos-aarch64, windows-x86_64
3. Create GitHub release with all binaries
4. Include SHA256 checksums
5. Sign binaries if possible

## Technical Approach
Use cross-rs for cross-compilation or cargo-zigbuild for simpler setup.

Workflow matrix:
- ubuntu-latest: linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64 (via cross)
- macos-latest: macos-x86_64, macos-aarch64 (native)
- windows-latest: windows-x86_64 (native)

## Files to Create
- .github/workflows/release.yml

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Push v0.1.58 tag triggers release build
- [ ] All 5 platform binaries attached to release
- [ ] SHA256 checksums file included
- [ ] Binaries are statically linked where possible
- [ ] Release notes auto-generated from commits","notes":"### Testing & Logging
- Unit: checksum verifier validates artifact naming + SHA256 manifest format.
- Integration: CI dry-run job builds artifacts and checksums, capturing logs as artifacts.
- E2E: release smoke check validates assets via GitHub API with clear error summary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:03:04.441231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:53.512142Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T23:44:53.512008Z","close_reason":"Merged into coding_agent_session_search-33xf (Release Artifacts section)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
